CVE Notify has flagged a critical vulnerability, CVE-2026-31059, lurking within the UTT Aggressive HiPER 520W router, specifically in its /goform/formDia component. According to CVE Notify, this flaw allows unauthenticated attackers to achieve remote command execution (RCE) by simply sending a specially crafted string to the vulnerable endpoint.

This is a classic case of input sanitization gone wrong. The vulnerability in the `formDia` interface appears to be a prime example of how insufficient validation can lead to serious security breaches. Attackers can leverage this weakness to inject and execute arbitrary commands on the affected device, potentially taking full control of the router's operations.
